data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run the tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

This project uses Microsoft Testing Platform (indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` being true). To collect crash dumps on test host crashes, I need to add the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` NuGet package and then pass the `--crashdump` option when running tests.

Let me add the package and run the tests.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-67f8f095b3db4637844a231cba566317/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-67f8f095b3db4637844a231cba566317/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-67f8f095b3db4637844a231cba566317/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-67f8f095b3db4637844a231cba566317/TestProject.csproj
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
+    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.2" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>

+    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.2" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag is a Microsoft Testing Platform flag, not a `dotnet test` flag in the traditional sense. With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` enabled, I need to pass it differently. Let me check how to pass testing platform options through `dotnet test`. The way to pass these options is with `--` separator or via the `--` prefix for the testing platform options.

Actually, with `dotnet test` and TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport, you pass the testing platform options using `--` separator:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Let me try that.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via -- separator

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I did:

1. **Added `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump`** NuGet package to `TestProject.csproj` — this is the Microsoft Testing Platform extension that enables crash dump collection.

2. **Run command**: `dotnet test -- --crashdump` — the `--` separator passes `--crashdump` to the testing platform. If the test host process crashes, a dump file will be automatically collected in the test results directory.
